title: Iran War Has Drained U.S. Supplies of Critical, Costly Weapons
summary: The Pentagon’s rush to rearm its Mideast forces makes it less ready to confront potential adversaries like Russia and China, administration and congressional officials say.

title: How the Iran War Is Morphing Into a Volatile Standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The conflict has morphed into a volatile standoff in the Strait of Hormuz, as the economic costs mount and President Trump faces a political backlash at home.

title: War and Sanctions Accelerate China’s Currency Push
summary: China’s bid to build a renminbi-based financial system beyond the U.S. dollar’s reach is gaining traction as a way to sidestep sanctions.

title: Trump’s Dreams for a Battleship Led to His Navy Secretary’s Ouster
summary: The Navy secretary, John Phelan, was supposed to deliver the first of the president’s ships by 2028. The timeline was nearly impossible.

title: Trump Reposts Anti-Immigrant Tirade Calling China and India ‘Hellhole’ Places
summary: The president touched off a furor with his post sharing a podcast episode in which the host referred to China and India as “hellhole” places.

title: Pentagon Fires Stars and Stripes Newspaper’s Ombudsman
summary: The newspaper’s ombudsman, Jacqueline Smith, said she had been given no reason for her dismissal.

title: Soldier Used Classified Information to Bet on Maduro’s Ouster, U.S. Says
summary: Federal prosecutors say that Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke, who was involved in the operation to oust Nicolás Maduro from power in Venezuela, used the information to place bets on a prediction market.

title: Concern Grows Over Republican Congressman’s Mysterious Absence
summary: A spokesman for Representative Thomas Kean Jr. said an unspecified “personal medical issue” has led the New Jersey Republican to miss House votes.

title: Dan Goldman Will Personally Match Each Donation as He Fights to Keep House Seat
summary: Daniel Goldman is pledging at least $1 million of his own money, and possibly millions more, in hopes of fighting off a primary challenge from Brad Lander in New York City.

title: DeepSeek’s Sequel Set to Extend China’s Reach in Open-Source A.I.
summary: Chinese companies have embraced making their most advanced artificial intelligence models available to all.

title: Meta to Lay Off 10 Percent of Work Force in A.I. Push
summary: The layoffs affect about 8,000 employees, with Meta also planning to close 6,000 open roles, as the company focuses on artificial intelligence.

title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s Epic Fight Heads to Court
summary: A jury trial that is set to start on Monday could shift the course of the A.I. race, with Mr. Musk seeking billions of dollars in damages from Mr. Altman’s OpenAI.

title: Intel’s Revenues Soar, Aided by A.I. Boom
summary: The chip maker reported a 7 percent rise to $13.6 billion in its latest quarter, more than $1 billion more than Wall Street expected.

title: Europe Mulls What Mutual Defense Looks Like Outside NATO
summary: European Union nations have a little-known obligation to protect one another. Experts caution it is no replacement for NATO.

title: Saudis Withdraw Offer of Millions to Metropolitan Opera
summary: The arrangement would have brought up to $200 million to the Metropolitan Opera, which has suffered a series of financial setbacks.

title: A Leaner Saudi Arabia Turns From Grandiose Plans to Pragmatism
summary: A decade after Mohammed bin Salman unveiled his “Vision 2030” program to transform the country’s economy, the kingdom is facing financial strains and reassessing its trajectory.

title: Touring Africa, Pope Leo Raised His Voice, but Didn’t Like the Echo
summary: On his recent trip abroad, Leo XIV made some of his most forthright comments since becoming pope last year, but grew uncomfortable at how that criticism was interpreted.

title: Communication Failures Preceded Deadly Crash at LaGuardia, N.T.S.B. Says
summary: The report focuses on the lack of transponders in the fire trucks, which investigators suggested could have allowed an automatic warning system to alert the controller of an imminent collision.

title: Two Litigation Partners Depart Paul Weiss
summary: Kannon Shanmugam and Masha Hansford are the latest in a string of litigators who have left the influential New York law firm.

title: Doug Allan, Polar Cameraman for David Attenborough’s Films, Dies at 74
summary: He was renowned for his skill at capturing candid scenes of penguins, polar bears and other cold-weather creatures — and for his ability to tolerate extreme discomfort.

title: New Gene Therapy Enables Children With a Rare Form of Deafness to Hear
summary: The treatment, the first of its kind, was approved by the Food and Drug Administration on Thursday. “Our baby was born deaf, and now he can hear,” said one parent.

title: Anne Hathaway, Queen of Effort, Is Finally Ready to Vibe
summary: Known best for tightly wound characters in generational hit films, the actress is a tortured pop star in “Mother Mary” and returns to playful form in “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”

title: Tim Cook Did Wonders for Apple. He Also Did a Lot for Xi Jinping.
summary: He made his company a juggernaut, but he did so by risking America’s economic and political security.

title: I’m One of Cuba’s Political Prisoners. When Will I Go Free?
summary: Nearly five years in Cuban prison have taught me that the government won’t loosen its hold on power.

title: Tornado Hits Enid, Oklahoma, as Severe Weather Threatens Central U.S.
summary: The tornado damaged homes and struck an Air Force base in Enid, Okla., the local sheriff said. Some people were injured.

title: In Britain, 7 Unelected Lords Are Helping to Block an Assisted Dying Bill
summary: A small group in the House of Lords has proposed hundreds of amendments that are helping stymie a bill that was meant to legalize medically assisted death for the terminally ill.

title: ‘Didi vs. Modi’: A Test for the Hindu Right in India’s Bengali Heartland
summary: The party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi has been making gains in a state once beyond its grasp, but many decry an audit that removed nine million voters, many of them Muslim.
